title: centaurea nigrescens subsp. serotina
intro: Centaurea nigrescens subsp. serotina, also known as late knapweed, is a flowering plant species in the genus Centaurea, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, offering various health benefits such as relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and aiding in respiratory issues. Centaurea nigrescens subsp. serotina can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
